name: Hong Kong Pork Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from the meat.
2. Place in a crock pot and season with salt, ginger, and pepper.
3. Drain syrup from pineapple chunks and add water to make 1 1/2 cups. Reserve some chunks for serving.
4. Add the drained pineapple syrup to the pork, stir in brown sugar, chicken broth, molasses, and vinegar.
5. Cover and cook on low for 8 hours or high for 4 hours.
6. Turn off heat. Combine cornstarch with 1/4 cup cold water and stir into the pork mixture until well blended.
7. Add cooked sweet potatoes, tomatoes, pineapple chunks, and green pepper cubes to the pot.
8. Cover and simmer on low for 20 minutes or until thickened and vegetables are heated through.
